methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $125M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Freeform is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Erik Palitsch that has raised $125M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $125M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B progression, yielding $238M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects material dispersion in robotics valuations at this stage, driven by differences in technology maturity, go-to-market traction, and market positioning.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $202M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $656M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound conservatively; the peer-median method suggests substantially higher valuations are supported in this cohort. Key uncertainties include Freeform's specific revenue profile and capital efficiency relative to peers, neither of which are provided in available data, and the application of the upper-quartile peers (1X at $10B) to a nascent company.
